definitions.text: A geometrical figure consisting of two straight lines or bars intersecting each other such that at least one of them is bisected by the other.
examples: Put a cross for a wrong answer and a tick for a right one.

definitions.text: Transverse; lying across the main direction.
examples: At the end of each row were cross benches which linked the rows.
His actions were perversely cross to his own happiness.

definitions.text: Opposing, adverse; being contrary to what one would hope or wish for.
The cross product of the previous vector and the following vector.
examples: The Lorentz force is q times v cross B.

definitions.text: To make or form a cross. To place across or athwart; to cause to intersect.
examples: She frowned and crossed her arms.

definitions.text: To place across or athwart; to cause to intersect.
examples: She frowned and crossed her arms.

definitions.text: To lay or draw something across, such as a line.
